steps.title: Find old versus new
steps.content: The claim says Archer's New York society preferred familiarity over novelty. The best quotation must show an available new option but emphasize continued satisfaction with an older, familiar one.

steps.title: Exclude unrelated opera details
steps.content: Choice A concerns the convention of not arriving early at the opera. Choice B concerns a performer's first appearance and the audience gathered to hear her. Choice C concerns arriving by hired carriage rather than one's own carriage.

steps.title: Choose the familiar venue
steps.content: Choice D contrasts talk of a new, grand opera house with society remaining content in the old Academy's familiar boxes. That directly illustrates preferring familiarity over novelty. The answer is D.
